topStories.headline: Historic July 4th Fireworks to Launch from Golden Gate Bridge — Pier 39 and Fisherman's Wharf Among Top Viewing Spots
topStories.dek: San Francisco celebrates America's 250th birthday with a once-in-a-generation fireworks display launched from the Golden Gate Bridge towers, with additional barges near Pier 39 making the North Waterfront a prime destination.
topStories.body:
San Francisco's annual Fourth of July fireworks show is moving from its usual Fisherman's Wharf location to the Golden Gate Bridge this year in a historic celebration of America's 250th anniversary. The free display, scheduled to begin at approximately 9:30 p.m. on Saturday, July 4, marks only the third time in the bridge's nearly 90-year history that fireworks have been launched from its iconic towers. The last bridge-based show was in 2012 for the span's 75th anniversary.

For North Waterfront residents and visitors, the celebration will be felt close to home. Fireworks will also launch from barges positioned near Pier 39 and Aquatic Park, making Fisherman's Wharf one of the best viewing areas in the city. KQED identified Pier 39 and the Northern Embarcadero among the top viewing locations. Free musical entertainment is scheduled from 2 p.m. to 9:15 p.m. at the Crab Wheel Plaza on Jefferson and Taylor streets.

Expect significant traffic impacts across the neighborhood. According to SFMTA and Axios, Jefferson Street between Hyde Street and the Embarcadero will be closed from 1 to 11 p.m. Streets north of Bay Street will be restricted to local access only from 8 to 11 p.m. The Golden Gate Bridge will be fully closed to all traffic from approximately 9 to 10 p.m. Officials strongly urge using public transit; Muni will run Marina fireworks shuttles from 4 to 11:30 p.m.

As many as 200,000 spectators are expected along the waterfront. The National Weather Service forecasts pleasant conditions but warns the usual coastal fog could be present.

topStories.headline: New Fisherman's Wharf Plaza Expected to Open This Summer on Former Alioto's Site
topStories.dek: The Port of San Francisco's $10 million Fisherman's Wharf Forward project is on track to deliver a new 5,000-square-foot public plaza where the storied Alioto's restaurant once stood.
topStories.body:
A new public plaza at the heart of Fisherman's Wharf is expected to open this summer as the centerpiece of the Fisherman's Wharf Forward project, the Port of San Francisco's $10 million initiative to revitalize the neighborhood. The plaza occupies the site of the former Alioto's Restaurant, which closed in 2022 after nearly a century in business and was demolished in late 2025.

According to the SF Port and the San Francisco Examiner, the 5,000-square-foot space will feature wave-shaped seating, picnic tables, and benches overlooking the colorful fleet of Monterey fishing boats in the inner lagoon, with views of the Golden Gate Bridge and San Francisco Bay. String lights, upgraded landscaping, and new signage celebrating the wharf's fishing heritage are also planned. A new inner lagoon overlook along Al Scoma Way is part of the near-term improvements.

Some community members have launched an effort to name the plaza after the Alioto family. Nunzio Alioto first opened the business 101 years ago as a fish stand along the water. The Port Commission is developing a formal naming policy for the space.

Meanwhile, new restaurants are filling vacancies nearby. According to the Fisherman's Wharf Advisory Committee, Oakland barbecue restaurant Everett & Jones is anticipated to open in summer 2026 in the former Lou's Fish Shack space, and a new Salvadoran seafood restaurant called Chasca Rio's is slated for the former Pompei's Grotto.

topStories.headline: Pier 39 World Cup Watch Parties Continue Through Tournament as Knockout Round Heats Up
topStories.dek: The Fisherman's Wharf landmark is hosting free "Soccer with the Sea Lions" screenings of FIFA World Cup matches on its outdoor video wall through the July 19 final.
topStories.body:
Pier 39 has become one of San Francisco's most popular destinations for FIFA World Cup 2026 watch parties, with free public screenings running through the tournament's July 19 final. The "Soccer with the Sea Lions" series broadcasts matches on the outdoor video wall at the Bay End of the pier, drawing fans from around the world to the waterfront.

The watch parties are part of the Bay Area Host Committee's network of more than 30 free fan zones across the region. CBS San Francisco reported that the USA vs. Türkiye group stage match at Pier 39 on June 25 drew enthusiastic crowds despite typically chilly San Francisco summer weather. Upcoming screenings include Round of 16 matches on July 5–7, quarterfinals on July 9–10, semifinals on July 14–15, and the final on July 19.

The World Cup excitement has provided a significant boost to the Fisherman's Wharf area, which earlier this year recorded one million visitors in a single month for the first time since the pandemic — a 24% increase over the same period in 2024. The Fisherman's Wharf Community Benefit District's approved Entertainment Zone, located between Polk and Powell streets and Beach Street to the Little Embarcadero, allows open-air alcohol consumption and adds to the festive atmosphere.
topStories.sourceName: SF Bay Area FIFA World Cup 26 Host Committee

events.title: Fourth of July Fireworks — Golden Gate Bridge & Fisherman's Wharf
events.location: Golden Gate Bridge, with additional barges near Pier 39 and Aquatic Park
events.description: San Francisco celebrates America's 250th anniversary with a historic fireworks display launched from the Golden Gate Bridge towers. Pier 39 and the Northern Embarcadero are among the best viewing spots. Free musical entertainment at Crab Wheel Plaza (Jefferson & Taylor) from 2–9:15 PM.
events.sourceName: Golden Gate Bridge, Highway and Transportation District

events.title: Fourth of July Waterfront Celebration at Pier 39
events.description: All-day celebration with live entertainment, family-friendly activities, restaurants and shops open. Fireworks viewing from the pier at 9:30 PM. Free admission.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Watch Parties — Soccer with the Sea Lions
events.description: Free public screenings of FIFA World Cup knockout round matches on the outdoor video wall at the Bay End of the pier. Upcoming matches include Round of 16 (July 5–7), quarterfinals (July 9–10), semifinals (July 14–15), and the final (July 19).
events.sourceName: SF Bay Area FIFA World Cup 26 Host Committee

events.title: Dear San Francisco — Immersive Circus Show at Club Fugazi
events.location: Club Fugazi, 678 Green Street, North Beach
events.description: The critically acclaimed immersive circus experience by The 7 Fingers continues its run in the heart of North Beach. The 90-minute production celebrates San Francisco's history through acrobatics, spoken word, and original music. Tickets from $86.90.

events.title: Fillmore Jazz Festival
events.location: Fillmore Street between Jackson and Eddy Streets
events.description: The largest free jazz festival on the West Coast returns for its annual July 4th weekend celebration. Features 30+ acts on three stages named after Tony Bennett, Miles Davis, and John Coltrane, plus food vendors, art, and pop-up bars across 12 blocks. Free admission.

events.title: Stern Grove Festival — Major Lazer
events.location: Sigmund Stern Grove, 19th Avenue & Sloat Blvd
events.description: Major Lazer headlines the free outdoor concert series with support from Fijiana and DJ Bad Juuju. Free tickets distributed via online lottery; advance registration required.

events.title: Fisherman's Wharf Pop-Up Fish Market
events.description: Weekly pop-up market offering locally caught, pre-cut seafood and cooked crab from the fishing fleet. Operated by the local fishing community with support from the Fisherman's Wharf Community Benefit District.

moreNews.headline: Fisherman's Wharf Tourism Rebounds — One Million Visitors Recorded in a Single Month
moreNews.body:
For the first time since the pandemic, Fisherman's Wharf recorded one million visitors in a single month earlier this year, a 24% increase over the same period in 2024, according to CBS San Francisco and the San Francisco Examiner. The milestone comes as the neighborhood undergoes a transformation through the Fisherman's Wharf Forward project and benefits from World Cup tourism.

However, for the second year in a row, Fisherman's Wharf also topped a survey for the most mentions of the term "tourist trap" in online reviews, according to data compiled by Nomad eSIM. Fisherman's Wharf Community Benefit District Executive Director Bri Maughan pushed back, calling the wharf "a dynamic, evolving neighborhood" where longtime institutions stand alongside new businesses.

moreNews.headline: Fisherman's Wharf Entertainment Zone Approved for Open-Air Festivities
moreNews.body: The Fisherman's Wharf Community Benefit District's Entertainment Zone has been officially approved by the City of San Francisco, allowing open-air alcohol consumption in the area between Polk and Powell streets and Beach Street to the Little Embarcadero. According to the Fisherman's Wharf Advisory Committee's March 2026 meeting notes, the district launched its first event in the new zone in May with an annual chowder competition. The zone is expected to host additional events throughout the summer World Cup season and beyond.

moreNews.headline: New Restaurants Coming to Fisherman's Wharf Waterfront
moreNews.body: Several new dining options are set to open at Fisherman's Wharf this year, filling storefronts left vacant during the pandemic. According to minutes from the Fisherman's Wharf Advisory Committee's March 2026 meeting, Oakland-based barbecue restaurant Everett & Jones is anticipated to open in summer 2026 in the former Lou's Fish Shack space and has already put up its sign. A Salvadoran seafood restaurant called Chasca Rio's is also expected to open by the end of 2026 in the former Pompei's Grotto. The Port is also working with Maven Real Estate to lease the café space at Fisherman's Grotto.
